description:
Kimi Claw is a browser-hosted OpenClaw AI agent that runs entirely in the cloud — no local setup, VPS, or always-on machine required.

In this video tutorial, I walk through what Kimi Claw is, how to set it up, and how its persistent agent model differs from traditional chat AI like ChatGPT or Claude. We’ll look at scheduled automations (cron jobs), ClawHub skills, and how hosted OpenClaw agents can run tasks and workflows for you in the background.

Kimi Claw is still in beta — especially around integrations and connectors — but it already shows how always-on AI agents can move from local infrastructure into the browser.

🎓 Skool community coming soon — exclusive content, direct access & Q&A. Founders lock in lowest pricing forever → https://snapperai.io/skool

⏱️ TIMESTAMPS

00:00 OpenClaw Agents in the Browser: Kimi Claw Intro
00:57 Deploying Kimi Claw (One-Click Setup)
01:37 Kimi Claw Agent Workspace Overview
02:04 Creating a Scheduled Automation (Cron Job)
03:40 Managing Scheduled Tasks
04:42 Telegram Integration & Security Model
05:52 ClawHub Skill Library Explained
06:23 Installing a Skill in Kimi Claw
07:12 Wrap-up & What’s Next

🔍 WHAT THIS VIDEO COVERS

◆ What Kimi Claw is and how it relates to OpenClaw
◆ Browser-hosted AI agents vs local runtimes
◆ Persistent agents, memory, and scheduled automations
◆ ClawHub skills and agent capabilities
◆ Security considerations in hosted agent platforms

🧪 IMPORTANT CONTEXT

Kimi Claw is currently in beta. Integrations like Telegram are configured conversationally and credential handling is not yet fully exposed in the UI.

Hosted agents like this should be treated more like SaaS automation platforms than local OpenClaw deployments where you control runtime and storage.
